data.title: GREEN: Implement UNLINK event handler in process_outbound()
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Description
Implement the UNLINK event handler as an elif branch in process_outbound() in bridge-outbound.py. Uses read-before-delete pattern to safely remove Jira Relates links.

## Implementation Details
1. Add elif event_type == "UNLINK": branch in process_outbound()
2. Filter: only process UNLINK events where data.relation == "relates_to"
3. Resolve source Jira key from SYNC file (existing pattern). If no SYNC, skip event
4. Resolve target Jira key from target ticket SYNC file. If no SYNC, write bridge_alert and skip
5. Read-before-delete: call acli_client.get_issue_links(source_jira_key)
   - Handle 404 from get_issue_links as "already gone" (source issue deleted from Jira) — skip silently
6. Match: find link where type.name == "Relates" AND (outwardIssue.key == target_jira_key OR inwardIssue.key == target_jira_key)
   - If no match found: skip (link already removed or never existed)
7. Delete: call acli_client.delete_issue_link(matched_link["id"])
   - Handle 404 on delete as "already gone" (concurrent deletion) — skip silently
   - Handle other CalledProcessError: write bridge_alert, continue processing
8. Write SYNC event for traceability

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-outbound.py (Edit: add UNLINK elif branch)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] process_outbound handles UNLINK events
  Verify: grep -q 'event_type == "UNLINK"' plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-outbound.py
- [ ] All RED tests from task 6428-6252 now pass
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py -k "unlink" --tb=short -q
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py --tb=short -q
